memes.label: "Appreciation"
memes.description: "The cognitive and affective capacity to recognize the intrinsic superiority of higher pleasures, which grounds Mill's qualitative hedonism."

memes.description: "Mill's concept of certainty in moral knowledge, contrasted with mathematical certainty."

memes.label: "Competent Judges"
memes.description: "The competent judges in Mill's qualitative hedonism whose experience qualifies them to rank pleasures."

memes.label: "Constitution"
memes.description: "The underlying structure of human nature — both emotional and cognitive — that Mill analyzes to explain the origin and force of moral sentiments including justice."

memes.label: "Estimation"
memes.description: "The process of estimating the value of pleasures, actions, and justice in Mill's utilitarianism."

memes.label: "Expectation"
memes.description: "The legitimate anticipation of treatment or outcomes that grounds the obligations of justice in Mill's utilitarian ethics."

memes.label: "Merit"
memes.description: "The moral quality of deserving good or evil based on conduct, which forms one of the core principles of justice that Mill derives from the idea of desert."

memes.label: "Morals"
memes.description: "Morals as a practical art in Mill's utilitarianism, grounded in the principle of utility."

memes.label: "Transcendental Moral Theory"
memes.description: "Mill's critique of transcendental moral theories that locate obligation outside human experience."

memes.label: "Well-Being"
memes.description: "The comprehensive state of human welfare that Mill identifies as the ultimate end of utilitarian morality and the proper object of the most binding moral rules."
